title: Stage-2 round-boundary roll TRIM: add a defensive max-iteration bound + escalating ALARM (mirror the entry build's max_adds)
why: The iterate-to-target ENTRY build is bounded by stage2_max_entry_adds (default 20) so a thin book can't make it chase fills forever. The round-boundary roll TRIM (execution/live_loop.py ~1318-1395) has no equivalent cap. It is PROVABLY CONVERGENT (each clamped trim sells >= min(|delta|,volume_max), monotonically decreasing until |delta|*px<$1; the section14 incomplete-gate holds-and-retries while over-safe, never locking in an over-safe basket) -- so it is NOT a livelock and NOT a launch-blocker for our brokers (PaperBroker never returns ok=True/fill_qty=0 on a reduce). The only 'stuck not advancing' path needs a live MT5 adapter that returns ok=True with a 0-qty fill on a reduce order, which would block-and-retry without ever advancing the round (stays section14-SAFE, just stuck).
value_rationale: LOW: defensive instrumentation, not a correctness fix. Deferred off the launch-night hot path deliberately.
scope: Add a max-trim-iterations counter on the roll's incomplete-gate path (mirror stage2_max_entry_adds).
On exhaustion: emit a loud escalating [ALARM] for operator intervention and HOLD (do NOT advance -- advancing over-safe is the section14 risk); do not silently retry forever.
Confirm the live MT5 adapter never returns ok=True with a 0-qty fill on a reduce order (else add a min-fill guard).
recommendation: Do AFTER launch (not on the delicate roll path tonight). The path is provably convergent for our brokers.
out_of_scope: No change to the convergent normal-case trim behavior.
